text:
-----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
Hash: SHA1

On 04/01/2013 15:19, Hiroki Sato wrote:
> Vincent Hoffman-Kazlauskas <vince@unsane.co.uk> wrote
> in <201301041447.r04ElB8L099496@red.freebsd.org>:
>
> vi> >Description:
> vi> The macro VIFM_ISEMPTY in vif.h defines
> vi> #define VIFM_ISEMPTY(m) ((m) == 0)
> vi> but was being used in prune.c with extra parentheses
> vi>
> vi> if (VIFM_ISEMPTY(gt->gt_grpmems))
> vi>
> vi> This was causing clang to error out with multiple
> vi> prune.c:1421:24: error: equality comparison with extraneous
> vi> parentheses [-Werror,-Wparentheses-equality]
> vi> if (((g->gt_grpmems) == 0))
>
> ...
>
> vi> >Fix:
> vi> I fixed it by changing the entries in prune.c, however i'm not much of
> vi> a programmer so it may be better to fix elsewhere. patch that works
> vi> for me(tm) attached.
>
> This warning should be suppressed because clang has an exception for
> macro expansion. Did you use ccache or something on your machine? I
> could not reproduce it on a -CURRENT box with the following clang
> version:
>
> |FreeBSD clang version 3.2 (tags/RELEASE_32/final 170710) 20121221
> |Target: x86_64-unknown-freebsd10.0
> |Thread model: posix
>
> -- Hiroki
Ahh yes I use ccache. It works fine if I set CCACHE_DISABLE. Possibly
Just change the Makefile to make it set CCACHE_DISABLE=1
Sorry for the noise.

Vince
